qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unvalidated $regex in query endpoint enables unauthenticated ReDoS against MongoDB
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies unvalidated $regex patterns in MongoDB query paths (MongoTransform.js, DatabaseController.js, ClassesRouter.js). The advisory describes JWT audience validation bypass in Google/Apple/Facebook authentication adapters. These are different vulnerable code locations, different vulnerability classes (ReDoS vs. improper authentication), and different root causes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The sole qualifying finding does not identify the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes a JWT audience validation bypass in the Google, Apple, and Facebook authentication adapters of parse-server, where missing clientId/appIds configuration causes audience validation to be silently skipped. Finding 'redos-unvalidated-regex-query' identifies a ReDoS vulnerability in MongoDB query handling via unvalidated $regex patterns in MongoTransform.js and DatabaseController.js. The finding involves a different vulnerability class (ReDoS vs. improper authentication), different vulnerable code, and a different root cause. No finding involves JWT verification, authentication adapters, or audience claim validation.
evidence: Advisory root cause: JWT audience validation silently skipped in Google/Apple/Facebook auth adapters when clientId/appIds not configured
Advisory vulnerable components: src/Adapters/Auth/google.js, src/Adapters/Auth/apple.js, src/Adapters/Auth/facebook.js (authentication adapter JWT verification)
Finding redos-unvalidated-regex-query root cause: No regex complexity validation in src/Adapters/Storage/Mongo/MongoTransform.js and src/Controllers/DatabaseController.js
